---
title: "Analyses"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
  html_notebook: default
  pdf_document: default
---

# Setup

```{r setup}

# Load packages
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, 
  knitr,
  patchwork,
  viridis, 
  gt,
  gtExtras,
  gtsummary,
  tidylog,
  lubridate,
  pheatmap,
  janitor,
  scales,
  lme4,
  broom.mixed,
  geepack,
  marginaleffects,
  splines,
  performance,
  geepack,
  DHARMa,
  car,
  sf,
  scatterpie,
  rnaturalearth,
  rnaturalearthdata,
  survival,
  survminer,
  gt,
  openxlsx
  )

setwd("K:/Sensitivt/Forskning/24-19410_Candidemistudie")

# Read the data
data <- read_csv2("Data/Full_data.csv")

# Merge preterm categories - too few in each of the preterm categories
data <- data %>%
  mutate(preterm = case_when(
    preterm == "Preterm" | preterm == "Extremely preterm" ~ "Combined preterm",
    TRUE ~ as.character(preterm)
  ))

# Ensure lopenr is a factor
data$lopenr <- as.factor(data$lopenr)

set_theme(theme_classic())

```

# Table 1

```{r table 1}

table1 <- data %>%
  select(mikro_kat2, age, age_cat, sex, has_cancer, preterm, transplantation, resistant,  fl_resistant, ec_resistant, health_trust) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = mikro_kat2,  
    type = list(
      age ~ "continuous",  
      age_cat ~ "categorical",
      has_cancer ~ "categorical",  
      preterm ~ "categorical",
      transplantation ~ "categorical",
      resistant ~ "categorical",
      fl_resistant ~ "categorical", 
      ec_resistant ~ "categorical",
      sex ~ "categorical",
      health_trust ~ "categorical"
    ),
    label = list(
      age ~ "Age",
      age_cat ~ "Age categories",
      has_cancer ~ "Haematological malignancy",
      preterm ~ "Preterm",
      transplantation ~ "Stem cell transplantation",
      resistant ~ "Any drug resistance",
      fl_resistant ~ "Fluconazole resistance", 
      ec_resistant ~ "Echinocandin resistance",
      sex ~ "Sex",
      health_trust ~ "Health trust"
    ),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "{median} ({p25}, {p75})",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
    ),
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 0,
    missing = "ifany",
    percent = "row"
  ) %>%
  add_overall() %>%  
  modify_header(label = "**Covariate**") %>%
  bold_labels()

table1

# Save it
# Convert to gt object
gt_table1 <- as_gt(table1)

# Save 
gtsave(gt_table1, "table1.docx")

```

# Table 2

```{r table 2}

# Create the age group variable
data <- data %>%
  mutate(age_group = if_else(age < 65, "Younger (<65 years)", "Older (≥65 years)"))

# Create Table 2
table2 <- data %>%
  select(age_group, mikro_kat6) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = age_group,
    type = mikro_kat6 ~ "categorical",
    statistic = mikro_kat6 ~ "{n} ({p}%)",
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
    missing = "ifany",
    label = mikro_kat6 ~ "Candida species"
  ) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  modify_header(label = "**Species**") %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 2: Distribution of Candida species by age group**") %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  bold_levels()

# Print the table
table2

# Save it
# Convert to gt object
gt_table2 <- as_gt(table2)

# Save 
gtsave(gt_table2, "table2.docx")

```

```{r heatmap}

# Prepare the data
heatmap_data <- data %>%
  filter(health_trust != "Unknown") %>%  # Remove unknown health trusts
  group_by(health_trust, mikro_kat6) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # Calculate proportions and total observations
  group_by(health_trust) %>%
  mutate(total = sum(count),
         proportion = count / total * 100) %>%
  ungroup()
  
heatmap_data$health_trust <- with(heatmap_data, reorder(health_trust, total))

# Create the heatmap with magma color scale
heatmap <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = mikro_kat6, y = health_trust, fill = proportion)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "plasma", direction = -1) +
  labs(x = "Candida species", y = "Health trust") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```


```{r map}

# Base map of Norway (projected)
norway <- ne_countries(
  country = "Norway",
  scale = "medium",
  returnclass = "sf"
) %>%
  st_transform(25833) %>%
  st_crop(
    xmin = -2e5,
    xmax = 1.6e6,
    ymin = 6.2e6,
    ymax = 8.0e6
  )

# Add coordinates
data_with_coords <- data %>%
  mutate(
    lon = case_when(
      health_trust == "Akershus universitetssykehus HF" ~ 10.57,
      health_trust == "Helse Nord-Trøndelag HF" ~ 11.30,
      health_trust == "Helse Stavanger HF" ~ 5.43,
      health_trust == "Helse Møre og Romsdal HF" ~ 6.15,
      health_trust == "Oslo universitetssykehus HF" ~ 10.44,
      health_trust == "Helse Bergen HF" ~ 5.19,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset i Vestfold HF" ~ 10.41,
      health_trust == "St. Olavs hospital HF" ~ 10.23,
      health_trust == "Vestre Viken HF" ~ 10.12,
      health_trust == "Universitetssykehuset Nord-Norge HF" ~ 18.57,
      health_trust == "Nordlandssykehuset HF" ~ 14.38,
      health_trust == "Sørlandet sykehus HF" ~ 7.59,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset Østfold HF" ~ 11.06,
      health_trust == "Helse Fonna HF" ~ 5.17,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset Innlandet HF" ~ 10.28,
      health_trust == "Helse Førde HF" ~ 5.86,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    lat = case_when(
      health_trust == "Akershus universitetssykehus HF" ~ 59.53,
      health_trust == "Helse Nord-Trøndelag HF" ~ 63.70,
      health_trust == "Helse Stavanger HF" ~ 58.58,
      health_trust == "Helse Møre og Romsdal HF" ~ 62.47,
      health_trust == "Oslo universitetssykehus HF" ~ 59.54,
      health_trust == "Helse Bergen HF" ~ 60.23,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset i Vestfold HF" ~ 59.27,
      health_trust == "St. Olavs hospital HF" ~ 63.25,
      health_trust == "Vestre Viken HF" ~ 59.44,
      health_trust == "Universitetssykehuset Nord-Norge HF" ~ 69.39,
      health_trust == "Nordlandssykehuset HF" ~ 67.28,
      health_trust == "Sørlandet sykehus HF" ~ 58.08,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset Østfold HF" ~ 59.17,
      health_trust == "Helse Fonna HF" ~ 59.26,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset Innlandet HF" ~ 61.70,
      health_trust == "Helse Førde HF" ~ 61.45,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(health_trust != "Unknown")

# Prepare health trust data
trust_data <- data_with_coords %>%
  group_by(health_trust) %>%
  summarise(
    lon = first(lon),
    lat = first(lat),
    albicans = sum(mikro_kat2 == "Candida albicans"),
    non_albicans = sum(mikro_kat2 == "Candida non-albicans"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(total = albicans + non_albicans)

# Convert to sf and project
trust_sf <- st_as_sf(
  trust_data,
  coords = c("lon", "lat"),
  crs = 4326
) %>%
  st_transform(25833) %>%
  mutate(
    x = st_coordinates(.)[, 1],
    y = st_coordinates(.)[, 2]
  )

# Manual nudges for South-East Norway
trust_sf <- trust_sf %>%
  mutate(
    x_plot = case_when(
      health_trust == "Oslo universitetssykehus HF" ~ x + 140000,
      health_trust == "Akershus universitetssykehus HF" ~ x,
      health_trust == "Helse Fonna HF" ~ x + 40000,
      health_trust == "Vestre Viken HF" ~ x - 30000,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset Østfold HF" ~ x + 70000,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset i Vestfold HF" ~ x + 20000,
      TRUE ~ x
    ),
    y_plot = case_when(
      health_trust == "Oslo universitetssykehus HF" ~ y + 60000,
      health_trust == "Akershus universitetssykehus HF" ~ y + 100000,
      health_trust == "Helse Fonna HF" ~ y - 8000,
      health_trust == "Vestre Viken HF" ~ y,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset Østfold HF" ~ y - 15000,
      health_trust == "Sykehuset i Vestfold HF" ~ y - 30000,
      TRUE ~ y
    )
  )

trust_plot <- trust_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry()

# Leader lines (from true centroid to nudged pie)
leader_lines <- trust_sf %>%
  filter(x != x_plot | y != y_plot)

# Map
map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = norway,
    fill = "grey95",
    colour = "grey60"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = leader_lines,
    aes(x = x, y = y, xend = x_plot, yend = y_plot),
    linewidth = 0.3,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  geom_scatterpie(
    data = trust_plot,
    aes(x = x_plot, y = y_plot, r = sqrt(total) * 2500),
    cols = c("albicans", "non_albicans"),
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = trust_plot,
    aes(x = x_plot, y = y_plot, label = str_remove(health_trust, " HF$")),
    size = 2.2,
    point.size = sqrt(trust_plot$total) * 2500 / 2800,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    segment.size = 0.25,
    box.padding = 0.4,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    seed = 42
  ) +
  coord_sf() +
  scale_fill_viridis(
  discrete = TRUE,
  name = "Species",
  labels = c(
    albicans = "Candida albicans",
    non_albicans = "Candida non-albicans"
  )
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
  )

map

```

```{r Norway figure}

# Create the combined plot using patchwork
combined_plot <- (map / heatmap) +  # Vertical arrangement
  plot_layout(heights = c(2, 1))  # Map gets 2/3 of space, heatmap 1/3

# Save the combined plot as PNG
ggsave(
  "candidaemia_map_heatmap.png",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 12,
  height = 14,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)

```

# Incidences

```{r incidences}

# Calculate crude incidence over time for each species and total
incidence_data <- data %>%
  group_by(year = year(prøvedato), mikro_kat6) %>%
  summarise(incidence = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Calculate total incidence
total_incidence <- data %>%
  group_by(year = year(prøvedato)) %>%
  summarise(total = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(total_scaled = total / 2)  # Scale down to match primary axis

trend_fit <- glm(total ~ year, data = total_incidence, family = poisson)
trend_p <- summary(trend_fit)$coefficients["year", "Pr(>|z|)"]
trend_label <- paste0("p for trend < ", formatC(trend_p, format = "f", digits = 3), "1")

incidence_a <- ggplot() +
  # Add species lines (primary axis)
  geom_line(data = incidence_data, aes(x = year, y = incidence, color = mikro_kat6), linewidth = 1.5) +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE, name = "Species") +
  # Add total line (secondary axis) 
  geom_line(data = total_incidence, aes(x = year, y = total_scaled),
            color = "grey20", linewidth = 2, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.4) +
  # Add p for trend
  annotate("text", x = 2020, y = max(total_incidence$total_scaled) * 0.95,
           label = trend_label, size = 3.5, hjust = 1, vjust = 10, colour = "grey20") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Species incidence",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~.*2, name = "Total incidence")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "grey20")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2008, 2024, by = 2))  

incidence_b <- ggplot(data, aes(x = age, fill = mikro_kat6)) +
  geom_density(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, name = "Species") +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Proportion") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10))

incidence_c <- ggplot(data, aes(x = year(prøvedato), fill = mikro_kat6)) +
  geom_density(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, name = "Species") +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Proportion") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2008, 2024, by = 2))  

# Combine the three graphs vertically with equal heights
combined_incidence <- (incidence_a / incidence_b / incidence_c) 

# Save the combined plot
ggsave(
  "combined_incidence.png",
  plot = combined_incidence,
  width = 10,
  height = 15,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)

```

```{r survival}

comparators <- c(
  "Candida tropicalis",
  "Candida glabrata",
  "Candida parapsilosis",
  "Others",
  "Candida krusei"
)

km_one_vs <- function(df, comparator, use_mikro_kat2 = FALSE) {

  if (use_mikro_kat2) {
    plot_df <- df %>%
      filter(mikro_kat2 %in% c("Candida albicans", "Candida non-albicans")) %>%
      mutate(group = mikro_kat2)

    title <- "Candida albicans vs non-albicans"

  } else {
    plot_df <- df %>%
      filter(mikro_kat6 %in% c("Candida albicans", comparator)) %>%
      mutate(group = mikro_kat6)

    title <- paste("Candida albicans vs", comparator)
  }

  # Drop comparisons with only one group
  if (n_distinct(plot_df$group) < 2) return(NULL)

  fit <- survfit(
    Surv(case_fatality_days, case_fatality) ~ group,
    data = plot_df
  )
  
  lr <- survdiff(
    Surv(case_fatality_days, case_fatality) ~ group,
    data = plot_df
  )
  lr_p <- pchisq(lr$chisq, df = length(lr$n) - 1, lower.tail = FALSE)
  p_label <- paste0("Log-rank p = ", formatC(lr_p, format = "f", digits = 3))

  n_groups <- n_distinct(plot_df$group)

    ggsurvplot(
    fit,
    data = plot_df,
    conf.int = TRUE,
    legend.title = "Species",
    palette = viridis(n_groups),
    title = title,
    legend.labs = unique(plot_df$group)
  )$plot +
    annotate("text", x = max(plot_df$case_fatality_days, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
             y = 0.05, label = p_label, hjust = 1, size = 3.5)
}

plots <- Filter(
  Negate(is.null),
  c(
    list(km_one_vs(data, comparator = NULL, use_mikro_kat2 = TRUE)),
    map(comparators, ~ km_one_vs(data, .x))
  )
)

km_plots <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2)

# Save the combined Kaplan-Meier plots
ggsave(
  "kaplan_meier_plots.png",
  plot = km_plots,
  width = 12,
  height = 16,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)

```

# Modelling

```{r prepare data}

data_clean_fat <- data %>% select(lopenr, year, case_fatality, age, sex, has_cancer, preterm, transplantation) %>% na.omit()
data_clean_res <- data %>% select(lopenr, year, resistant, age, sex, has_cancer, preterm, transplantation) %>% na.omit()
data_clean_alb <- data %>% mutate(mikro_kat2 = ifelse(mikro_kat2=="Candida albicans", 0, 1)) %>% select(lopenr, year, mikro_kat2, age, sex, has_cancer, preterm, transplantation) %>% na.omit()

```

```{r table 3A and figure 1A}

# Run the best model (model_2)
model_best <- geeglm(case_fatality ~ ns(age, df = 2) + sex + year + has_cancer + preterm + transplantation,
                    data = data_clean_fat,
                    family = binomial,
                    id = lopenr,
                    corstr = "independence")

# Create predictions at each age point
age_predictions <- avg_predictions(model_best, newdata = datagrid(age = seq(0, 90, by = 10)),
                              by = FALSE)

# Plot the spline function
age_predictions_fine <- avg_predictions(model_best, newdata = datagrid(age = seq(0, 90, by = 1)),
                              by = FALSE, conf_level = 0.95)
plot_data <- data.frame(age = seq(0, 90, by = 1),
                        pred = age_predictions_fine$estimate,
                        conf.low = age_predictions_fine$conf.low, 
                        conf.high = age_predictions_fine$conf.high) 

plot_fat <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = age, y = pred)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.2, fill = viridis(1)) +
  geom_line(color = viridis(1), size = 1) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Predicted probability of case fatality") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 90, by = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
  scale_colour_viridis()

```

```{r table 3B and figure 1B}

# Run the model with natural cubic splines (df = 4)
model_best_res <- geeglm(resistant ~ ns(age, df = 4) + sex + year + has_cancer + preterm + transplantation,
                        data = data_clean_res,
                        family = binomial,
                        id = lopenr,
                        corstr = "independence")

# Create predictions at each age point
age_predictions_res <- avg_predictions(model_best_res, newdata = datagrid(age = seq(0, 90, by = 10)),
                                  by = FALSE)

# Plot the spline function with finer grid
age_predictions_fine_res <- avg_predictions(model_best_res, newdata = datagrid(age = seq(0, 90, by = 1)),
                                       by = FALSE, conf_level = 0.95)
plot_data_res <- data.frame(age = seq(0, 90, by = 1),
                            pred = age_predictions_fine_res$estimate,
                            conf.low = age_predictions_fine_res$conf.low,
                            conf.high = age_predictions_fine_res$conf.high)

plot_res <- ggplot(plot_data_res, aes(x = age, y = pred)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.2, fill = viridis(1)) +
  geom_line(color = viridis(1), size = 1) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Predicted probability of resistance") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 90, by = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
  scale_colour_viridis()

```

```{r table 3C and figure 1C}

# Run the model with natural cubic splines (df = 2)
model_best_alb <- geeglm(mikro_kat2 ~ ns(age, 2) + sex + year + has_cancer + preterm + transplantation,
                        data = data_clean_alb,
                        family = binomial,
                        id = lopenr,
                        corstr = "independence")

# Create predictions at each age point
age_predictions_alb <- avg_predictions(model_best_alb, newdata = datagrid(age = seq(0, 90, by = 10)),
                                  by = FALSE)

# Plot the spline function with finer grid
age_predictions_fine_alb <- avg_predictions(model_best_alb, newdata = datagrid(age = seq(0, 90, by = 1)),
                                       by = FALSE, conf_level = 0.95)
plot_data_alb <- data.frame(age = seq(0, 90, by = 1),
                            pred = age_predictions_fine_alb$estimate,
                            conf.low = age_predictions_fine_alb$conf.low,
                            conf.high = age_predictions_fine_alb$conf.high)

plot_alb <- ggplot(plot_data_alb, aes(x = age, y = pred)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.2, fill = viridis(1)) +
  geom_line(color = viridis(1), size = 1) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Predicted probability of C. non-albicans") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 90, by = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
  scale_colour_viridis()

```

# Collate results

```{r table 3}

# Create a combined table of predictions
combined_predictions <- data.frame(
  age = rep(seq(0, 90, by = 10), 3),
  outcome = rep(c("case_fatality", "resistant", "mikro_kat2"), each = 10),
  estimate = c(age_predictions$estimate,
               age_predictions_res$estimate,
               age_predictions_alb$estimate)
)

# Create a nicer table
kable(combined_predictions, caption = "Predicted probabilities at different ages for each outcome",
      digits = 3, align = c('c', 'l', 'c'))

# Save it
write.xlsx(combined_predictions,
           "combined_predictions.xlsx",
           sheetName = "Predictions",
           colNames = TRUE,
           rowNames = FALSE)

```

```{r figure 1}

# Save the combined plots
ggsave(
  "modelled_curves.png",
  plot = (plot_fat / plot_res / plot_alb) +
    plot_layout(heights = c(24, 24, 24)),
  width = 10,
  height = 15,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)

```

```{r model diagnostics}

# Create a function to get model diagnostics
get_diagnostics <- function(model) {
  # Get the model data
  model_data <- model.frame(model)

  # 1. Residual plots
  plot(resid(model, type = "pearson") ~ fitted(model),
       main = "Pearson Residuals vs Fitted",
       xlab = "Fitted values", ylab = "Pearson residuals")

  # 2. VIF for multicollinearity
  # Note: This is approximate for GEE models and produces warnings about it
  vif_values <- vif(glm(fitted(model) ~ ., data = model_data, family = binomial))
  print(vif_values)

  # 3. Goodness-of-fit using DHARMa
  sim_res <- simulateResiduals(model)
  plot(sim_res)

  # 4. Predicted vs observed
  pred_obs <- data.frame(
    observed = model.response(model.frame(model)),
    predicted = predict(model, type = "response")
  )
  plot(pred_obs$observed ~ pred_obs$predicted,
       main = "Observed vs Predicted",
       xlab = "Predicted", ylab = "Observed")
}

# Apply to each model
get_diagnostics(model_best)
get_diagnostics(model_best_res)
get_diagnostics(model_best_alb)

```

```{r episode analysis}

# Check for multiple episodes, new candidaemias with new species, and new candidaemias with same species
episode_analysis <- data %>%
  group_by(lopenr) %>%  # Patient ID
  summarise(
    total_episodes = n_distinct(prøvedato),  # Count episodes by sample date
    unique_species = n_distinct(mikrobe_std),  # Count unique species
    first_species = first(mikrobe_std)  # Get first species
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    has_multiple_episodes = total_episodes > 1,
    has_new_species = unique_species > 1
  ) %>%
  group_by(has_multiple_episodes, has_new_species) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    episode_type = case_when(
      has_multiple_episodes & has_new_species ~ "Multiple episodes with new species",
      has_multiple_episodes & !has_new_species ~ "Multiple episodes with same species",
      !has_multiple_episodes & has_new_species ~ "Single episode with multiple species",
      !has_multiple_episodes & !has_new_species ~ "Single episode with single species",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

# Print the results
print(episode_analysis)

```
